messages.content: The paper is published in Chinese, but later published in English. How to define the two languages with the same topic? Do they belong to different publications

In this case, the two versions of the paper can be considered as separate publications, each in its respective language. The Chinese version would be considered as the initial publication, and when it is translated and published in English, it would be treated as a separate publication.

While both versions discuss the same topic, the language difference can result in variations in writing style, structure, and potentially even content. It is common for researchers to publish their work in multiple languages to reach a wider audience, since not all researchers and readers can understand every language. Translating and publishing the paper in English serves the purpose of making the research accessible to a broader international community.

Typically, each version of the paper would have its own unique identifier, such as a DOI (Digital Object Identifier) or an ISSN (International Standard Serial Number). This enables each version to be cited and referenced independently.

please help proofread the following paragraphs: Firstly, please allow me to provide the timeline of the incident including the 10 years’ background introduction and moods throughout the whole process. 

The involved FYP paper was written in Chinese in 2014 under my supervision. I do have 9 students to supervise that year and this is one of the best. During the 10-month supervision and thesis writing, I had a lot of discussions with the student (Mr Wang Yueming) and provided numerous comments and suggestions for this FYP's revision. The core point of view and used selected texts were set up through many pleasant conversations and emails with the student. When marking, I gave a 90 out of 100 for this project. After the submission, I talked to the student that the paper is meaningful and deserved to be published formally. I told him I may use the point of view and texts to write an English academic paper to submit to a journal. He agreed to do so. After long-time preparation and revisions, in 2018, the English article was published by a journal entitled Academics in China under my author. From my perspective, I shall say there is not pure plagiarism here because I did not purposefully steal the idea and materials from FYP or my student (will elaborate on my contribution to this FYP later), actually, I endorsed the FYP and hope the academic value could be more universally recognized in the academia. I believe this is the common wish for both me and my student. 

I must point out that I was authorized by the student to rewrite an article in English using FYP-relevant materials and statements and publish it which he was aware of. Therefore, there is no deliberately plagiarized behavior. However, I must admit that there is indeed some academic misconduct, which should take the student as a second author since he contributed a lot to the Chinese article, but unfortunately, I did not do so, I should apologize to the student and the University. I made the mistake at a moment under great pressure of promotion. I sincerely hope to get forgiveness from everyone.

Firstly, allow me to provide a timeline of the incident, including a 10-year background introduction and the emotions experienced throughout the entire process.

The FYP paper in question was originally written in Chinese in 2014 under my supervision. I had a total of nine students to supervise that year, and this particular paper stood out as one of the best. Over the course of 10 months of supervision and thesis writing, numerous discussions took place between myself and the student, Mr. Wang Yueming. I provided extensive comments and suggestions for revisions on this FYP. The core viewpoints and selected texts were established through many enjoyable conversations and email exchanges with the student. In grading the paper, I awarded it a score of 90 out of 100. Upon submission, I personally spoke to the student, expressing my belief that the paper held significance and deserved formal publication. I also informed him of my intention to use the viewpoints and texts to write an English academic paper for submission to a journal. The student agreed to this proposal. After extensive preparation and revisions, the English article was eventually published in 2018 by the journal "Academics in China," with myself listed as the author. From my perspective, I believe this does not constitute pure plagiarism, as I did not intentionally steal ideas or materials from the FYP or the student. On the contrary, I supported the FYP and aimed to promote its academic value on a broader scale. I believe this was a shared aspiration between myself and the student.

I must highlight that I received authorization from the student to rewrite and publish an article in English using relevant materials and statements from the FYP, which he was fully aware of. Therefore, there was no deliberate plagiarism involved. However, I must acknowledge that there was indeed some form of academic misconduct in not crediting the student as a second author, considering his significant contributions to the Chinese article. I deeply regret this oversight and would like to apologize to the student and the University. It was a mistake made in a moment of immense pressure for career advancement. I genuinely hope to be forgiven by everyone involved in this matter.

proofread please: After the allegations were released by social media - Reddit - and I was asked to give responses by the journalists (15:10pm and 16:12pm on 26 July separately) via emails. I immediately on the evening of 26 July contacted the student to explain the issue and sincerely apologize to him for my misconduct and concern of potential media violence. The student recalled that a long time ago I told him I wrote an English paper about Ruan Ji based on the FYP and I told him the paper was already published. During the conversation, the student well understood my standing and accepted my apology. We together recalled his university campus life, especially the FYP period, which is the most pleasant period in his life so far. We felt happy to see we had been keeping a very good relationship even after graduation. I need to confess here: I contacted him because the student has the right to know the allegation happened as he had been involved, I can confirm that all we talked is to recall details about the FYP and the paper. After reporting the issue to Chair, Prof Neil Murphy on 27 July, I was advised not to contact the student anymore and I did not contact him from then on.

After the allegations were released on social media platform Reddit, I was approached by journalists via email to provide responses. The exchanges with the journalists took place at 15:10pm and 16:12pm on 26 July, respectively. Upon learning of these allegations, I immediately contacted the student later that evening to address the issue and offer a sincere apology for my misconduct and concerns regarding potential media backlash. It was mentioned during our conversation that some time ago, I had informed the student about writing an English paper on Ruan Ji based on the FYP, and that I had already published the paper. Throughout our discussion, the student displayed an understanding of my perspective and accepted my apology. We reminisced about his university campus life, particularly the FYP phase, which he regarded as the most enjoyable period of his life thus far. We were glad to see that our relationship had remained strong even after his graduation. I must confess here that I reached out to him primarily because he had the right to be informed about the allegations considering his involvement. I can confirm that our conversation solely revolved around recollecting details about the FYP and the associated paper. On 27 July, I reported the matter to Chair, Prof Neil Murphy, who advised me against further contact with the student. Consequently, I refrained from reaching out to him thereafter.

proofread: Due to the disputable case, I decided to take down the paper from my Academia profile, revise my publication list on NTU website on the late night of 26 July to avoid further misunderstanding, and wait for the investigation from University. Prof Nei Murphy asked me to step back from all the exposures including the 27 July’s graduation students’ photo shoot and Convacation which I believe the University wanted to protect me confronting with the press.

Due to the controversial nature of the case, I made the decision to remove the paper from my Academia profile and revise my publication list on the NTU website late at night on 26 July. This was done in order to prevent any further misunderstandings and to await the investigation conducted by the University. Additionally, Prof Neil Murphy advised me to step back from any public appearances, including the graduation students' photo shoot and Convocation on 27 July. I believe this was a measure taken by the University to protect me from potential interactions with the press.

proofread: From 26 July, deeply bothered by this kind of internet bullying and attacking from social media and presses, like a sudden nightmare and misfortune, I cannot eat and sleep for the past few days and was close to a mental breakdown after seeing the many mainstream media reports without the investigation from the University. My personal life got messed up. Not only has it caused great harm to me, but it has also caused great distress to my family (my wife and two primary school students). I had to go for help from UWO Support and Care team on the morning of 28 July which relieved my worst mood a bit, however, the University HR and Chair asked me to come over the campus on the afternoon of 28 July to give me a RELIEF OF DUTIES AND EXCLUSION NOTICE asking me to return all items including office keys and laptops, block my email access immediately, this is a second suddenly great blow to my health and mind, resulting in I had nothing support from the University which is another great break. I am still NTU faculty member, right? Until the afternoon of 29 July, under Prof Neil Murphy’s help, I was again contacted by UWO colleague. I greatly appreciate his comfort and help at this difficult time.

Since 26 July, I have been deeply disturbed by the internet bullying and attacks from social media and the press. It feels like a sudden nightmare and unfortunate situation. As a result, I have been unable to eat or sleep for the past few days and have been on the brink of a mental breakdown after witnessing numerous mainstream media reports without any investigation conducted by the University. My personal life has been thrown into disarray. Not only has it caused severe harm to me, but it has also caused immense distress to my family, including my wife and two young children who are in primary school. On the morning of 28 July, I sought assistance from the UWO Support and Care team, which provided some relief from my deeply troubled state of mind. However, in the afternoon of the same day, the University's HR department and Chair asked me to come to the campus, where I was handed a Notice of Relief of Duties and Exclusion. I was instructed to return all University-related items, including office keys and laptops. Furthermore, my email access was promptly blocked. This sudden development dealt a severe blow to my health and mental well-being, leaving me without any support from the University, adding to the strain I was already under. I want to clarify that I am still a faculty member at NTU, am I not? On the afternoon of 29 July, thanks to the assistance of Prof Neil Murphy, a UWO colleague reached out to me once again. I am immensely grateful for their comfort and support during this challenging time.

proofread: Secondly, I want to elaborate on a few thoughts or observations of this issue.
1.My contribution to the FYP: 
1)Help of selection of Topic and Theory: from the first semester of BA, the student listened to my course about Ruan Ji and approached to me to ask whether Ruan Ji and Escapism can be further researched.  
2)Help of finding out the references including works of Ruan Ji and comparing author (Tao Yuanming) and related research papers and works
3)Help of building up the Frame structure: it's very important to guide the student to put the ideas in a suitable place and form a logic narrative. That's why I did not change the frame structure in the "case studies" parts of the English paper. 
Language proofreading and editing: I do this for every student when they submit the draft to me. For the FYP, I edited a few times with comments and suggestions.

Secondly, I would like to elaborate on a few thoughts and observations regarding this issue.

1. My contribution to the FYP:
   a) Assistance in Topic and Theory Selection: From the first semester of the student's Bachelor's program, he attended my course on Ruan Ji and approached me to inquire about the possibility of further research on Ruan Ji and Escapism.
   b) Assistance in Reference Finding: I helped the student in identifying relevant references, including works by Ruan Ji and other related authors such as Tao Yuanming, as well as research papers.
   c) Assistance in Building the Structure: Guiding the student in organizing ideas and forming a logical narrative is crucial. That's why I retained the frame structure in the "case studies" sections of the English paper, as it was originally established.
   d) Language Proofreading and Editing: I provide language proofreading and editing support for all my students when they submit drafts to me. For the FYP, I made several edits with comments and suggestions.

I believe these contributions are reflective of typical supervision and guidance provided to students during their research process.

proofread: 1.How to calculate the supervisor's contribution to FYP?
According to the student's acknowledgment, we had a lot of discussions and even debates, many good ideas and thoughts came up and we together put them into the FYP through he drafted and I revised, especially the case for Chapter 3: "Ruan Ji's escapism complex: case studies". That's why I think this part could be published separately. To be frank, by that time, I was young and not clear about the FYP authorship which is certainly my fault. My idea is quite simple: I contributed a lot to this topic and I truly cherished the idea of this article as the student so I tried to convert it into a different platform.  I started to rewrite the paper in English in 2014, including the newly starting paragraphs and the conclusion, after almost 4 years, it was eventually published. I didn't want to plagiarize, otherwise, I will not upload the article online, on the contrary, I should hide it safely if I committed this behavior to be plagiarized.

1. How to determine the supervisor's contribution to the FYP?

Based on the student's acknowledgment, we engaged in numerous discussions and even debates, which generated many valuable ideas and thoughts that were incorporated into the FYP. The student drafted the content, and I provided revisions, particularly in the case studies section of Chapter 3, titled "Ruan Ji's escapism complex." I believe this portion of the FYP holds enough significance to be published as a separate entity. I must honestly admit that, at that time, I was young and not fully aware of the authorship protocol surrounding FYPs, which is undoubtedly my fault. My intentions were simple: I had made substantial contributions to this topic, and I held a genuine appreciation for the idea behind this article. Hence, I attempted to transform it into a different format. I commenced rewriting the paper in English in 2014, including new introductory paragraphs and concluding remarks. After nearly four years, it was eventually published. I had no intention to plagiarize; otherwise, I would not have uploaded the article online. On the contrary, I would have concealed it if I were engaged in any plagiaristic behavior.

In my opinion, the two versions of the paper in Chinese and English can be considered as separate publications, each in its respective language. The Chinese version would be considered as the initial publication, and when it is translated and published in English, it would be treated as a separate publication. It is common for researchers to publish their work in multiple languages to reach a wider audience, since not all researchers and readers can understand every language. Translating and publishing the paper in English serves the purpose of making the research accessible to a broader international community. Additionally, FYP is not considered a formal published research paper. As I mentioned earlier, for the second part of the paper, I contributed a lot to the structure frame and words editing in Chinese so I did not make further changes when converted it into English.

1. How to distinguish the two language editions?

In my perspective, the Chinese and English versions of the paper should be regarded as distinct publications, each in its respective language. The Chinese version can be seen as the original publication, while the translation and publication in English represent a separate entity. It is quite common for researchers to disseminate their work in multiple languages to cater to a broader audience, considering that not all scholars and readers possess fluency in every language. The translation and publication of the paper in English facilitate its accessibility to the international academic community. Usually, each version of the paper would possess its unique identifier, such as a DOI (Digital Object Identifier) or an ISSN (International Standard Serial Number). These identifiers allow for separate citation and referencing of each version.

Furthermore, it is important to note that an FYP is not typically considered a formally published research paper. As I mentioned earlier, in the second part of the paper, I played a significant role in contributing to the structural framework and language editing in Chinese. Consequently, when the paper was translated into English, I did not make substantial changes since the underlying structure remained intact.

proofread: 3.Some suspicion about this issue: 
1)Why now: after the releasing the appraisal results in early July, before the Convocation and Orientation regardless of University's reputation
2)Where to get the resource. It must be from internal because the so-called proofs and comments are so detailed 
3)Who did: Not only including our students but some faculty may be involved as well 
4)What purpose: some political interest involved although Head of Chinese is not a senior admin position (I was very hesitant to take up this role because I know it's quite complicated in the department and actually occurred some objections when the Chair wanted to appoint me), someone wants to break me down by this chance 
5)Example: event of 28 March with a senior faculty from Chinese programme

3. Some suspicions regarding this issue:

1) Timing: It raises questions as to why these allegations surfaced now, shortly after the release of the appraisal results in early July and just before the Convocation and Orientation events, potentially impacting the University's reputation.

2) Source of information: The level of detail in the presented proofs and comments suggests that the information may have originated internally within the University.

3) Potential involvement: It is possible that individuals beyond just our students may be involved in this matter, including faculty members.

4) Potential motives: There could be underlying political interests at play, even though the Head of Chinese is not a senior administrative position. It is worth noting that I initially had reservations about accepting this role due to the complexities within the department, including objections raised when the Chair wanted to appoint me. This raises concerns that someone may be attempting to undermine me through this opportunity.
